issues: Recent failures cluster inside the same spine; pivot layers instead of replaying the spine
Recent failures cluster inside the same phenotype family; widen quote assembly before another replay

baseline-split-side-protection.expected_failure_signature: If calm windows cheapen both sides together or arb_loss_to_retail_gain regresses, the split still behaves like a replay.
baseline-split-side-protection.expected_win_condition: Recover weak calm slices while keeping one-sided toxicity protection selective and defensive by default.
baseline-split-side-protection.hidden_coupling_removed: The incumbent uses one additive surface to set both the symmetric defensive floor and the toxic-side surcharge.
baseline-split-side-protection.why_not_coefficient_retune: This changes quote assembly into two explicit channels instead of retuning one shared surface.

baseline-split-side-protection-v2.expected_failure_signature: If mean fees stay elevated without another step-up in low-retail and low-volatility slices, the floor is still too sticky; if arb leakage rises, the skim is too permissive.
baseline-split-side-protection-v2.expected_win_condition: Increase mean_edge materially over screen_0002 by letting the non-toxic side win more calm flow while the toxic side remains explicitly protected.
baseline-split-side-protection-v2.hidden_coupling_removed: The first split still keeps too much hazard spend in the shared floor, muting the asymmetry advantage of the toxic-side overlay.
baseline-split-side-protection-v2.why_not_coefficient_retune: This keeps the two-channel assembly but changes which channel owns hazard spend and how much baseline flex the non-toxic side may reclaim.

baseline-split-side-protection-v3.expected_failure_signature: If high-decile and overall edge do not rise after harder toxic-side charging, the line is too muted to clear margin; if arb-loss ratio rises, the floor has been cut too far.
baseline-split-side-protection-v3.expected_win_condition: Lift mean_edge materially above screen_0003 while staying under the fee guardrail and keeping low-retail at or above its new frontier.
baseline-split-side-protection-v3.hidden_coupling_removed: Even after v2, too much event and hazard spend is still trapped in the shared floor instead of charging the toxic side directly.
baseline-split-side-protection-v3.why_not_coefficient_retune: This keeps the same two-channel topology but reassigns more protective spend from the baseline floor to the toxic overlay while holding the skim discipline fixed.

baseline-split-side-protection-v4.expected_failure_signature: If fee spend stays elevated and retail edge does not recover, the line is overspending; if raw edge collapses quickly, the overlay was carrying the result and the split is not robust.
baseline-split-side-protection-v4.expected_win_condition: Stay near screen_0004 on mean_edge while bringing time-weighted mean fee and retail edge back toward the incumbent.
baseline-split-side-protection-v4.hidden_coupling_removed: The harder toxic overlay now buys raw edge, but retail recovery is still tied too directly to the same fee spend, so reclaim must happen through a narrower non-toxic path.
baseline-split-side-protection-v4.why_not_coefficient_retune: This keeps the two-channel split but changes how retail recovery is reintroduced after the harder toxic overlay, instead of globally relaxing the whole surface.

baseline-split-side-protection-v5.expected_failure_signature: If fee spend and retail edge remain pinned near screen_0004, the calm-window gate is still too weak; if raw edge drops back toward screen_0003, the reclaim leaked outside true non-toxic windows.
baseline-split-side-protection-v5.expected_win_condition: Stay near screen_0004 on mean_edge while reducing time_weighted_mean_fee and pulling retail_edge back above 540.
baseline-split-side-protection-v5.hidden_coupling_removed: screen_0004 still forces recovery cuts to stay uniformly tight even in confirmed calm windows, so non-toxic reclaim cannot reappear without reopening the full surface.
baseline-split-side-protection-v5.why_not_coefficient_retune: This preserves the screen_0004 split but changes when the recovery path reactivates by tying the recovered rebates to the existing calm-window gate, rather than loosening both sides globally.

earned-calm-credit-ledger.expected_failure_signature: If calm credits refill too easily, the book drifts into the same broad cheapening and arb leakage as the weak-slice envelope line.
earned-calm-credit-ledger.expected_win_condition: Target low-retail and low-decile improvement without changing the incumbent defensive floor.
earned-calm-credit-ledger.hidden_coupling_removed: Calm relief is granted by the same contemporaneous signals that decide broad protection, so spend persists longer than evidence supports.
earned-calm-credit-ledger.why_not_coefficient_retune: This changes how opportunity spend is accrued and exhausted instead of softening thresholds on the same overlay.

filtered-cluster-state.expected_failure_signature: If jumps remain noisy or the filter over-smooths into tail_protection_loss, the new state semantics are not buying useful control.
filtered-cluster-state.expected_win_condition: Smooth protective behavior without flattening the defensive floor into an arb leak.
filtered-cluster-state.hidden_coupling_removed: Instantaneous shocks currently drive both volatility estimation and release timing.
filtered-cluster-state.why_not_coefficient_retune: This changes state semantics and memory persistence while keeping quote assembly fixed.

hazard-gated-side-regime.expected_failure_signature: If the regime switch flaps or both sides still move together, the branch will look like another over-spiky replay.
hazard-gated-side-regime.expected_win_condition: Charge the toxic side more selectively while preserving the incumbent calm baseline on the non-toxic side.
hazard-gated-side-regime.hidden_coupling_removed: One controller currently decides both when the book should become defensive and how one-sided toxicity should be charged.
hazard-gated-side-regime.why_not_coefficient_retune: This introduces a risk-semantic regime switch rather than another additive overlay or cheap router.

recommended_next_batch.reason: Exploit the strongest raw survivor screen_0004 before widening again.
Arb leakage remains a distinct failure mode; keep one branch directly targeting arb_edge or arb_loss_to_retail_gain.
Keep one branch focused on weak low-retail, low-volatility, or tail slices instead of only overall mean_edge.
Keep one branch with explicit quote-selectivity and fee-discipline guardrails so structural pivots fail fast.
Reserve one orthogonal pivot so the batch does not collapse into semantically different versions of the same phenotype.
